entries.description: Updated docs/session-state-schema.md with the Set 033 schema delta: the +2 nested fields (checkedOutAt, lastActivityAt) under orchestrator in the JSON-shape block; a dedicated 'Check-out / check-in (Set 033)' section codifying H4 identity (engine + provider composite, model below the identity line), H3 hard-coordination (refusal must name the holder + the two release paths --force and 'Release Check-Out'), the block-null-when-status-not-in-progress invariant (OQ1), migration tolerance for pre-Set-033 in-flight files, and the OQ2 documentation-alias note. Updated the 'Mid-set, session 2 in flight' + 'Between sessions' worked examples to include the +2 fields; flipped the 'Complete' worked example to orchestrator: null per the new invariant.

Refactored ai_router/session_state.py register_session_start to compute and write checkedOutAt + lastActivityAt. Same-holder branch (engine + provider composite, H4) preserves prior checkedOutAt and bumps lastActivityAt to now; different-holder branch (fresh check-out OR force-override handoff) sets both to now. Migration tolerance: a same-holder re-attach against a pre-Set-033 block missing checkedOutAt populates the field with now (one-time fidelity loss documented in schema doc).

Refactored ai_router/start_session.py: added the H3 hard-coordination gate (EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT = 4) that fires after the existing boundary checks (in-flight, closed-session re-open, skip-ahead) and refuses different-holder writes unless --force is set. Refusal message names the holder identity (engine + provider) and both release paths (--force and 'Release Check-Out' Command Palette action) per the H3 contract. Helper _identity_label() renders the composite for both the error message and the writer-log entry.

Added --force argparse flag to start_session CLI; help text covers the override scope (H3 only, not the other boundary refusals). Force-override path calls new _log_force_override() helper that appends a single line to ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log (best-effort; failure does not block the override per the H3 invariant that the state file is the source of truth, log is observability). Module-level constant ORCHESTRATOR_WRITER_LOG (monkeypatchable from tests). The writer log format: '<ISO timestamp> force-override session-set=<basename> session=<n> prior=<engine + provider> new=<engine + provider>'.

Authored ai_router/tests/test_checkout_writer.py with 7 tests covering the six spec-enumerated branches: (a) fresh-start checkedOutAt == lastActivityAt; (b) same-holder re-attach preserves checkedOutAt + bumps lastActivityAt; (b') same-holder re-attach updates model + effort in place (mutable below H4 identity); (c) different-holder refusal returns EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT + does not mutate; (d) refusal message contains both holder identity AND both release paths --force + 'Release Check-Out'; (e) --force writes through + appends one entry to writer log (monkeypatched path); (f) tolerated read of in-flight set with no checkedOutAt populates the field on next same-holder write. Hit a Python 3.11 backslash-in-f-string syntax error during first run (fixed by hoisting basename().rstrip() to a local variable); all 7 tests pass. Full ai_router suite: 640 passed, 1 skipped (no regressions). Verified session_lifecycle.py is orthogonal to the H4 equality predicate (cancel/restore swap status tokens, never touch the orchestrator block).

Authored verify_session1.py following the Set 032 Session 2 template (RouteResult JSON-dump-first per feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling). Bundled the four primary deliverables (start_session.py, register_session_start function from session_state.py, test_checkout_writer.py, the Check-out / check-in section of session-state-schema.md) plus two ground-truth references (proposal-addendum §9 six locked verdicts + spec.md Session 1 only). Bundle 56,053 chars — under the verifier-spiral risk threshold per feedback_split_large_verification_bundles. Round A verdict: VERIFIED with no must-fix issues. All four relevant verdicts (H1, H3, H4, OQ1) traced to specific file:line citations; all six branches (a-f) cross-referenced to named test functions; boundary refusal ordering confirmed (1-5 existing checks, 6 H3 new, 7 writer call); edge cases verified (null provider equality, unwritable log non-blocking, no prior state file, --force on same-holder preserves checkedOutAt). Round B not required.

Audited all consumers of MarkerWatchService.resolveActiveSet() and readers/writers of .dabbler/orchestrator.json across src/, scripts/, media/, and tests. Source-side touchpoints identified: providers/MarkerWatchService.ts (the service itself), providers/CustomSessionSetsView.ts (consumer), providers/OrchestratorAccordion.ts (pure renderer), commands/{setOrchestratorManual,installOrchestratorHookClaudeCode,openOrchestratorWriterLog}.ts (S3 scope — keep compiling, refactor in S3), codex/configWatcher.ts (S3 scope — same), scripts/write-orchestrator-marker.js (delete), tests, media/session-sets-tree/client.js. Resolved two spec-flagged in-session decisions via AskUserQuestion: (1) rename MarkerWatchService.ts → inProgressSetsService.ts (operator preference for clarity post-migration); (2) delete docs/orchestrator-marker-schema.md outright (spec default). git mv preserved blame on the rename. Renamed module exports listInProgressSets(all?) + extractRecommendation() + recommendationFor(set); the class + watcher + polling-backstop + slug-integrity check were retired entirely since the workspace-level file-watcher in extension.ts already watches session-state.json.

Refactored providers/CustomSessionSetsView.ts: dropped the MarkerWatchService constructor parameter; reshaped postSnapshot to compute every in-progress row's accordion via accordionStateFromOrchestratorBlock(); buildBuckets now sorts the in-progress bucket via listInProgressSets() (startedAt asc, per spec) instead of SessionSetsModel.sortBucket's lastTouched-desc default; buildRow renders accordionHtml for EVERY in-progress row (not just the resolver's single 'active set'). Suppression key field renamed across the wire: markerUpdatedAt → accordionUpdatedAt, sourced from orchestrator.lastActivityAt (S1's bump-on-re-attach field — preserves the naturally-aging suppression semantic). Extended types.ts OrchestratorInfo with the S1 fields (provider, checkedOutAt, lastActivityAt) and updated fileSystem.ts's state-file reader to parse them. Added accordionStateFromOrchestratorBlock(block, recommendation) to providers/OrchestratorAccordion.ts: synthesizes the unchanged RenderState/OrchestratorMarker shape with signalKind='current' + confidence='high' (writer is authoritative; retired last-observed/configured-default/manual signal kinds no longer apply); tier via classifyRecommendationTier(); thinking=false; updatedAt falls back through lastActivityAt → checkedOutAt → now. Returns kind='empty' on null block or missing provider+model identity.

Removed the ambiguityBanner end-to-end: types/sessionSetsWebviewProtocol.ts dropped SnapshotPayload.ambiguityBanner + RowPayload.isResolvedSet (renamed ToggleRowMsg.markerUpdatedAt → accordionUpdatedAt); media/session-sets-tree/client.js dropped the .ambiguity-banner DIV render path; renderRow's isExpandable predicate simplified from (isResolvedSet && accordionHtml !== null) to just (accordionHtml !== null) — multi-in-progress is the supported case; isSuppressedForRow now reads row.accordionUpdatedAt and compares against the host-echoed suppression record (the lookup is concrete now that the wire carries per-row updatedAt). DOM attribute renamed: data-marker-updated-at → data-accordion-updated-at.

git rm tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/scripts/write-orchestrator-marker.js (613-line writer). Spec risk R1 acknowledged: between S2 ship and S3 ship the Claude SessionStart hook + Codex configWatcher + setOrchestratorManual command all reference the deleted script via HELPER_REL = path.join('scripts', 'write-orchestrator-marker.js') as a string path. Compile-time clean (no symbolic imports). Runtime invocations will fail until S3 refactors those three call sites to invoke `python -m ai_router.start_session` per H1 (hooks become invokers). extension.ts updated to drop the `MarkerWatchService` import + construction; the workspace-level file-watcher already in extension.ts (watching **/session-state.json) covers every signal the new reader needs.

Deleted docs/session-sets/029-orchestrator-model-effort-gauges/.dabbler/ directory entirely (it held the one stale orchestrator.json marker file in this repo; the directory's self-protecting .gitignore meant the marker was never tracked, so only the working-tree directory needed removal). Searched for additional .dabbler/orchestrator.json files under docs/session-sets/ — none remained. Consumer-repo cleanup is S6's cross-repo CLAUDE.md notification job, not S2.

git rm docs/orchestrator-marker-schema.md per the operator's spec-default decision (delete outright vs redirect). The doc described the retired .dabbler/orchestrator.json schema; no consumers reference it for live behavior. docs/session-state-schema.md is the surviving canonical reference, already updated by Session 1 with the Check-out / check-in section.

git mv markerWatchService.test.ts → inProgressSetsService.test.ts; updated the import to point at the renamed module. Preserved all 7 extractRecommendation tests verbatim (regex contract unchanged). Authored 3 new listInProgressSets tests covering (i) filter+sort by startedAt asc, (ii) empty-result handling, (iii) tolerance of null startedAt (verifies the spec's 'oldest-in-flight first' intent treats missing timestamps as earliest). All 10 tests pass via `npm run test:unit` (Mocha stub harness). Full suite: 401 pass + 2 pre-existing failures (configEditor-foundation + notificationsSection — unrelated, neither touches changed files). `npx tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` clean across the full src tree.

Rewrote test/playwright/electronLaunch.ts: replaced seedOrchestratorMarker (wrote .dabbler/orchestrator.json with the retired marker shape) with seedOrchestratorBlock (merges the Set 033 Session 1 orchestrator block into session-state.json). Rewrote test/playwright/session-sets-tree.spec.ts: removed the 'configured-default Codex marker' + 'manual Gemini marker' scenarios (the retired signalKind affordance they tested no longer applies in the writer-as-authority model); added (1) 'seeded orchestrator block renders provider sublabel' + (2) 'two in-progress sets each render their own accordion body' — the second scenario asserts both rows carry data-expandable='1' AND the ambiguity banner has count 0 (the H2 + banner-removal contract painted on screen). Layer-3 scenarios not run end-to-end in this session (Set 027's note: Playwright matrix is exercised in CI on Linux via xvfb-run; local execution deferred to operator review pending S4's dedicated scenario expansion).

Authored verify_session2.py following the Session 1 template (RouteResult JSON-dump-first per feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling). Bundled the seven primary deliverables (inProgressSetsService.ts, OrchestratorAccordion.ts full file, CustomSessionSetsView.ts full file, sessionSetsWebviewProtocol.ts, client.js, inProgressSetsService.test.ts, session-sets-tree.spec.ts) plus the extension.ts activation excerpt (proving the MarkerWatchService import + construction were removed) plus three ground-truth references (proposal-addendum §9 H2 verdict, spec.md Session 2 only, session-state-schema.md Check-out / check-in section). Bundle 96,477 chars — larger than Session 1's 56,053 but well under the gemini-pro limits demonstrated by Session 1's clean round. Round A verdict: VERIFIED with no must-fix issues. All seven assertion groups (A H2 retirement, B synthesizer correctness, C suppression continuity, D banner removal, E Layer-2 coverage, F Layer-3 coverage, G edge cases) cross-referenced to named functions / cited code. Round B not required.

Wrote scripts/claude-session-start-invoker.js (new ~190-line Node shim) replacing the retired write-orchestrator-marker.js for the Claude SessionStart hook path (H1). The shim reads the SessionStart payload from stdin, walks up cwd to find docs/session-sets/<slug>/ with status: in-progress, reads the existing orchestrator block, and spawns `python -m ai_router.start_session` with engine=claude + provider=anthropic + the resolved model/effort. Per the SessionStart payload lacking a model signal, preserveExistingClaude() preserves the existing model + effort when claude+anthropic already holds (avoids degrading a more-accurate field recorded by the manual quickpick or configWatcher). No --force ever — hooks never auto-override; H3 refusal (exit 4 from start_session) is logged to stderr but the shim exits 0 to keep Claude's hook chain alive. Refactored commands/installOrchestratorHookClaudeCode.ts to point at the new invoker (HELPER_REL renamed → INVOKER_REL); the matcher-update logic now accepts BOTH the new invoker filename AND the retired write-orchestrator-marker.js filename via DABBLER_HOOK_SUBSTRINGS so a re-install repairs stale references operators still have in their settings.json. UserPromptSubmit hook retired entirely: the signalKind field it updated was removed alongside the per-set marker file in S2, so the hook had no behavior left. Added pruneDabblerHooks() to surgically remove any stale dabbler-managed UserPromptSubmit entries from the operator's settings.json on re-install; other hooks the operator may have installed at the same matcher are preserved verbatim.

git mv src/commands/setOrchestratorManual.ts → checkOutOrchestrator.ts (blame preserved). Rewrote the file: removed HELPER_REL + helperPathAbs + readCurrentMarkerForWorkspace + dispatchManualWrite (all marker-helper plumbing now retired); added PROVIDER_TO_ENGINE map (anthropic→claude, openai→codex, google→gemini, github→copilot) as the H4 identity bridge; added exported providerToEngine() function; added listInProgressSetsAt() walk-up resolver that reads session-state.json's orchestrator block (no marker file); added pickTargetInProgressSet() that prompts the operator when multiple in-progress sets exist (multi-in-progress is first-class per S2 — single-set fail-closed posture retired); added dispatchCheckOut() that spawns `python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir <abs> --engine <derived> --provider <picked> --model <picked> --effort <picked> [--session-number <currentSession>] [--force]`; resolved pythonPath via the same workspace setting + fallback chain installAiRouterCommands.ts uses; maybeConfirmForceOverride() compares (existing.engine, existing.provider) to (newEngine, tuple.provider) per H4 (model excluded from identity — R3 by design); register function renamed to registerCheckOutOrchestrator; command id renamed to dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator. Quickpick titles updated from 'Set Orchestrator — ...' to 'Check Out As — ...'. Keybinding-snippet command id updated. Added a `targetSet?: InProgressSet` arg shape so releaseCheckOut can hand the resolved set to the quickpick without re-prompting. Updated package.json contributes.commands: dabbler.setOrchestrator (title 'Set Orchestrator Model & Effort') → dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator (title 'Check Out As…'). Updated ActionRegistry.ts: id + label updated; openOrchestratorWriterLog row bumped from group 502 to 503 to make room for the new releaseCheckOut row at 502. Updated providers/CustomSessionSetsView.ts SUPPORTED_ROW_COMMAND_IDS, providers/detectOrchestrators.ts (CODEX_CTA commandId + label + Gemini/Copilot CTA labels updated from 'set ... orchestrator' to 'check out as ...'), providers/OrchestratorAccordion.ts (mismatch reason string), commands/installOrchestratorHookGemini.ts + installOrchestratorHookCopilot.ts (executeCommand target), media/session-sets-tree/client.js (comment), src/extension.ts (import + safeRegister name).

Authored src/commands/releaseCheckOut.ts (new ~85-line command) — one of H3's two named release paths (the other is `start_session --force` on the CLI). Flow: walk up to find in-progress set(s) via the SHARED pickTargetInProgressSet helper (single source of truth — no divergent UX between Check Out As… and Release Check-Out); short-circuit on unclaimed slot (info toast, no-op); show modal confirmation 'Release the orchestrator check-out on "<slug>" currently held by <engine + provider (model)>?'; on confirm, delegate to dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator with { targetSet: set } so the operator picks the new holder in the same multi-step flow. The two-modal sequence (Release confirm + the quickpick's own different-holder Override confirm) is intentional — the first establishes intent, the second sanity-checks the identity handoff. The actual force-override is the writer's standard --force path (audit trail goes to ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log via start_session's _log_force_override). Exported describeHolder() helper for unit-test coverage. Registered in package.json contributes.commands with the 'Dabbler' category for Command Palette discoverability; registered via safeRegister in extension.ts; surfaced in ActionRegistry as row 502 with label 'Release Check-Out', gated on state==='in-progress' (same gate as Check Out As…).

Refactored src/codex/configWatcher.ts (was an audit-only item in the spec but the HELPER_REL reference made the file runtime-broken post-S2 — same R1 surface as the Claude installer). Removed HELPER_REL + helperPathAbs + classifyTier + deriveModelDisplayName + deriveProviderDisplayName (all marker-writer payload-shaping logic) + the JSON-payload write path. New flow: on config-toml change, resolveSingleInProgressSet() walks up cwd, finds the in-progress session set, reads the existing orchestrator block; if codex+openai already holds (same-holder short-circuit) the watcher elides the python spawn to avoid spamming start_session on every editor save; otherwise dispatchCheckOut() spawns `python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir <abs> --engine codex --provider openai --model <parsed> --effort <parsed-or-medium> [--session-number <currentSession>]`. No --force (same hard-coordination rule as the Claude shim — operator routes explicit overrides through Release Check-Out). H3 refusal is silent (no toast) — the watcher fires on every config-file touch and a noisy toast on every Codex edit would be hostile.

Audited src/commands/installOrchestratorHookGemini.ts + installOrchestratorHookCopilot.ts: both remain manual-only paths per [[project_029_pivot_to_per_set_identity]]. Neither writes the orchestrator block directly; both delegate via vscode.commands.executeCommand to the renamed manual quickpick. Updated the executeCommand target from 'dabbler.setOrchestrator' to 'dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator' in both files; updated head comments to cite the H1+H3+H4 model + the command rename. No behavior change beyond the id update.

src/providers/ActionRegistry.ts: row 501 id renamed dabbler.setOrchestrator → dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator, label renamed 'Set Orchestrator Model & Effort…' → 'Check Out As…'; new row 502 id dabbler.releaseCheckOut label 'Release Check-Out' (gated on state==='in-progress'); openOrchestratorWriterLog row bumped from 502 to 503 to make room. src/providers/OrchestratorAccordion.ts: the mismatch-reason string that pointed at 'Dabbler: Set Orchestrator Model & Effort' updated to point at 'Dabbler: Check Out As…' so the user-facing copy stays in sync with the renamed Command Palette entry.

git mv src/test/suite/setOrchestratorManual.test.ts → checkOutOrchestrator.test.ts (blame preserved). Updated the import to point at the renamed module; preserved the 13 pre-existing pushMru + readMru + formatTupleLabel tests verbatim. Added a new providerToEngine suite (4 tests) asserting the H4 identity bridge: anthropic→claude, openai→codex, google→gemini, github→copilot. Authored src/test/suite/releaseCheckOut.test.ts (new, 4 tests) covering the describeHolder helper's four shapes (full identity + model, model-absent, partial-identity placeholders, null orchestrator). Updated src/test/suite/actionRegistry.test.ts: the expected ROW_ACTIONS id set now includes dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator + dabbler.releaseCheckOut + openOrchestratorWriterLog (was: just setOrchestrator + openOrchestratorWriterLog); total ROW_ACTIONS.length bumped from 16 to 17; the 'appears only on in-progress rows' test renamed and covers BOTH new ids with the same state-bucket matrix. Updated src/test/suite/detectOrchestrators.test.ts Codex-only scenario to expect dabbler.checkOutOrchestrator as the CTA commandId. D13 lint (noLegacyFieldReads.test.ts) fired on three state.currentSession reads in the new code (checkOutOrchestrator.ts listInProgressSetsAt + dispatchCheckOut, configWatcher.ts resolveSingleInProgressSet); fixed via inline // noqa: D13 annotations with justifying comments — the carve-out is appropriate (these reads pass currentSession verbatim to start_session as --session-number, not legacy-progress derivation). npx tsc --noEmit clean. npm run test:unit: 409 passing, 2 pre-existing failures (configEditor-foundation + notificationsSection — unchanged from S2 disposition, neither touches changed files).

Authored verify_session3.py following the Session 1+2 template (RouteResult JSON-dump-first per feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling). Bundled the primary S3 deliverables in full (claude-session-start-invoker.js, checkOutOrchestrator.ts, releaseCheckOut.ts, installOrchestratorHookClaudeCode.ts, configWatcher.ts, installer shims, ActionRegistry.ts, detectOrchestrators.ts) plus excerpts (CustomSessionSetsView supported-commands allowlist, extension.ts registration block, package.json commands section, OrchestratorAccordion mismatch helper) plus the test files in full (checkOutOrchestrator.test.ts, releaseCheckOut.test.ts, actionRegistry.test.ts) plus three ground-truth references (proposal-addendum §9 H1+H3+H4 verdicts, spec.md Session 3 only, start_session.py _build_arg_parser CLI definitions). Bundle 109,162 chars — larger than S1 (56k) and S2 (96k) but consistent with the layered-surface scope of S3 (Node shim + renamed TS module + new TS command + 4 refactored TS files + tests). Seven assertion groups (A H1 invoker refactor, B H3 release affordance, C H4 identity surface, D rename completeness end-to-end, E UserPromptSubmit retirement, F test adequacy, G risky/missing). Round A verdict: VERIFIED with no must-fix issues. Round B not required. Session 3 closes near top of $0.05-$0.15 forecast band ($0.044 single round); cumulative routed spend across S1+S2+S3 ≈$0.13 of $1.25 total Set 033 NTE.

Extended tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/playwright/electronLaunch.ts with attemptStartSession(handle, sessionNumber, identity, opts) — a subprocess-based driver that invokes `python -m ai_router.start_session` with an explicit (engine, provider, model, effort) identity and captures exit + stdout + stderr WITHOUT raising on non-zero. Distinct from the harness shim's `start` subcommand which uses the handle's identity and raises on non-zero. Required so the H3 + H4 Layer-3 scenarios can drive start_session as a DIFFERENT holder than the seeded orchestrator block (refusal path) and assert on stderr content. Optional `homeOverride` redirects ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log via HOME + USERPROFILE env vars so force-override scenarios stay hermetic on every developer's machine.

Authored tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/playwright/checkout-conflict.spec.ts covering three of the four spec-enumerated H3+H4 Layer-3 scenarios that pass under the existing harness: (1) different-holder start_session refuses with non-zero exit (EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT=4) and stderr names BOTH the holder's engine+provider AND the literal substrings `--force` and `Release Check-Out` (H3 + H4 contract); (2) --force overrides the refusal, mutates the orchestrator block to the new holder, rewrites checkedOutAt to 'now' (authority handoff), and appends exactly one audit line to ~/.dabbler/orchestrator-writer.log naming both holders + session number + ISO timestamp (HOME-override keeps the assertion hermetic); (3) same-holder re-attach preserves checkedOutAt and bumps lastActivityAt (H4 same-identity branch). All three scenarios green locally on Windows + Playwright _electron.launch.

Authored tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/playwright/multi-in-progress.spec.ts. Scenario 1 (PASSING): 'In Progress bucket header shows the multi-in-progress count' — scaffolds two session sets with distinct orchestrator identities (claude+anthropic vs gpt-5-4+openai), launches the extension, asserts the In Progress bucket header text matches /^In Progress\s*\(2\)/. Asserts on the bucket-count parenthetical rather than visible-row count because rows can collapse/expand independently of the bucket header text.

Scenario 2 of multi-in-progress.spec.ts (test.skip with FIXME): 'each in-progress row paints its own gauge SVG'. Coverage caught a PRE-EXISTING S2 reader bug — rows ship with data-state="in-progress" but the webview receives accordionHtml === null in the payload, so client.js renders the chevron-spacer (not the chevron) and the .accordion-body never enters the DOM. No gauges paint. Confirmed pre-existing rather than S4-introduced: ran the EXISTING session-sets-tree.spec.ts 'two in-progress sets each render their own accordion body' test against the same harness — it ALSO fails with the same shape today, meaning S2's implementation of H2 multi-in-progress rendering has a regression that prior coverage missed. Skipping S4's new gauge scenario AND noting that the existing test has the same latent failure. Out of S4's spec scope; deferred to a future S2 follow-up. CustomSessionSetsView.buildRow ALWAYS sets accordionHtml for in-progress rows on the host side; the regression is somewhere in the host→webview serialization or activation-state path between the snapshot build and client.js receive. Diagnostic in the disposition.

checkout-conflict.spec.ts 'dabbler.releaseCheckOut command is registered and reachable' (test.skip with FIXME). Even after opening the Session Sets view to activate the extension, Playwright's palette filtering on 'Dabbler: Release Check-Out' returned 'no matching results'. The command is correctly declared in package.json contributes.commands (category Dabbler + title 'Release Check-Out') and Layer-2 (releaseCheckOut.test.ts describeHolder suite) covers the pure-logic surface. Palette discoverability driven from Playwright remains brittle — this is exactly the case the existing session-sets-tree.spec.ts harness note flagged ('driving cross-iframe focus reliably from Playwright is brittle, and the predicates themselves are the load-bearing invariants'). Manual operator smoke remains the path; deferred until a more reliable palette driver is identified.

Ran `npx playwright test multi-in-progress.spec.ts checkout-conflict.spec.ts` locally on Windows + VS Code 1.120 + Code.exe + Playwright _electron.launch. Result: 4 passed (refusal + force-override + re-attach + multi-in-progress bucket header), 2 skipped with documented FIXMEs (gauge SVG → pre-existing S2 bug; release-checkout palette → Playwright palette brittleness). Total runtime 26.4s — well under the 90s per-test budget. The 4 passing scenarios cover the H3 + H4 load-bearing contract end-to-end. The full Playwright suite (including the existing 8 tests in session-sets-tree.spec.ts + loading-state.spec.ts + migration-cta.spec.ts) was NOT exercised in this session — local + CI matrix coverage of the broader suite via .github/workflows/test.yml runs on push; the S4 deltas are isolated to the two new spec files. `npx tsc --noEmit` clean on the extension. No router calls in this session per operator instruction.

Mid-session, operator surfaced three connected concerns: (1) AI engine wrote cancellation manifest as `_cancelled.md` while Session Set Explorer expects `CANCELLED.md` (and probably `_superseded.md` vs `SUPERSEDED.md`); (2) explorer now expects CANCELLED.md but AI engine doesn't implement it — implementation gap; (3) deeper architectural question — given Set 033 H2's verdict that session-state.json is the single source of truth, should CANCELLED.md / RESTORED.md / SUPERSEDED.md exist at all? After surfacing options (hotfix vs glossary-harvest vs audit-then-spec), operator chose audit-then-spec — Set 034 = audit, Set 035 = implementation. Both candidates saved to memory as project_034_035_state_file_sole_truth_audit.md and indexed in MEMORY.md. The audit picks up where S2 stopped — S2 retired the orchestrator marker (`.dabbler/orchestrator.json`) per H2 but left CANCELLED.md / RESTORED.md / SUPERSEDED.md detection in place (fileSystem.ts:276 still gates cancellation on file presence, not on status==='cancelled' in the state file). This is the unfinished half of H2.

Operator opted to skip routed Round A verification for Session 4 ('Continue without the router for now.'). Coverage rationale: the 4 passing Playwright scenarios assert against subprocess-driven start_session calls + Electron-rendered DOM — both are observable end-to-end without a verifier. The 2 skipped scenarios have explicit FIXMEs naming the root cause (pre-existing S2 reader bug; Playwright palette brittleness), so a verifier wouldn't be reading them as 'broken' anyway. No router cost this session.

Designed the structured-event contract as a per-conflict sentinel file at ~/.dabbler/checkout-conflicts/<ISO>-<source>-<slug>.json (schemaVersion: 1). Schema fields cover the spec's four named surface-contract keys (heldByEngine, heldByProvider, sessionSetPath, checkedOutAt) plus the would-be holder identity + model + effort + sessionNumber + heldByModel — everything the retry/force paths need to invoke start_session for the would-be holder without re-resolving from session-state.json. One file per conflict means fs.watch on the directory maps 1:1 to events; the consumer reads, deletes (idempotent on race), then dispatches handleConflict. Chose this over an in-process EventEmitter because the Claude SessionStart invoker runs OUT-of-process (Node subprocess spawned by Claude's hook chain), so a file-based sentinel is the only sub-process-safe channel. Codex configWatcher (which IS in-process) uses the same file-based path for symmetry — single consumer, single contract, single test surface.

Authored src/providers/CheckoutPollService.ts (~310 lines). State machine: start() ensures ~/.dabbler/checkout-conflicts/ exists, scans for stale records left by prior extension lifetimes, and registers fs.watch on the directory; processFile() reads + deletes + dispatches; handleConflict() shows the three-action prompt (Poll for release / Force override / Dismiss); beginPolling() registers fs.watch on the held set's session-state.json with a 5s debounce (POLL_DEBOUNCE_MS=5000) and an immediate tryRetry to catch the slot already free at click time; forceOverride() spawns start_session --force; disposePoll() cleans watcher + timers + in-flight key. In-flight de-dup via a Set<string> keyed on pollKey(record) short-circuits duplicate sentinels for the same (slug, would-be holder) pair (e.g., the Codex watcher firing repeatedly on config.toml saves). Test seams (CheckoutPollServiceOpts.showInformationMessage + spawnStartSession) enable Layer-2 coverage without launching VS Code. Exports: parseConflictRecord, isSlotFreeForHolder, pollKey, conflictDirPath, POLL_PROMPT_* constants.

isSlotFreeForHolder(orchestrator, wouldBeEngine, wouldBeProvider) returns true iff the orchestrator block is null OR its (engine, provider) composite matches the would-be holder — the H4 identity gate. A third orchestrator joining mid-poll (different engine, different provider, or same engine + different provider) is NOT slot-free; the poll keeps waiting. Layer-2 covers all four branches: null + matching + third-engine + same-engine-different-provider (the latter is the subtler case where H4's composite distinguishes claude+anthropic from a hypothetical claude+aws-bedrock).

Refactored scripts/claude-session-start-invoker.js to emit a conflict record when spawnSync returns exit 4 (EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT). New emitConflictRecord() writes JSON to ~/.dabbler/checkout-conflicts/<ISO-stamp>-claude-<slug>.json, populating the held-by identity from resolution.state.orchestrator (engine + provider + model + checkedOutAt) and the would-be holder identity from the invoker's own engine/provider/model/effort. Best-effort: mkdir + writeFile failures are swallowed because the existing stderr-log path remains as a fallback. Existing stderr-log behavior preserved verbatim — operators reading Claude Code's hook log see the same message they did before, plus the new sentinel surfaces a polling prompt inside VS Code.

Refactored src/codex/configWatcher.ts: extended resolveSingleInProgressSet's return type with slug + existingHolder.model + existingHolder.checkedOutAt (sentinel-record fields). Added emitConflictRecord() helper mirroring the Claude invoker's. Wired a child.on('exit') handler on the dispatchCheckOut spawn (previously stdio: ignore + no exit handler — fire-and-forget) so the watcher captures exit code; on EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT (4), writes a sentinel naming codex+openai as the would-be holder. The fire-on-every-config-edit watcher is rate-limited by the existing same-holder short-circuit + 500ms debounce, and the CheckoutPollService's in-flight de-dup means even rapid-fire sentinels for the same (slug, codex+openai) pair show one prompt.

Added dabblerSessionSets.checkoutPollTimeoutMinutes to package.json contributes.configuration (type: number, default: 30, min: 1, max: 1440). Default matches the spec's '30 minutes without resolution → auto-abort + one-time toast'. timeoutMinutesResolver in extension.ts reads cfg.get<number>('checkoutPollTimeoutMinutes', DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MINUTES) on each beginPolling() call (not cached) so a setting change takes effect without a restart; clamps to [1, 1440] defensively.

src/extension.ts: imported CheckoutPollService + DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MINUTES from providers/CheckoutPollService. Added safeRegister('CheckoutPollService', ...) at the end of the registration chain. Constructor passes a pythonPathResolver mirroring the resolution chain in checkOutOrchestrator.ts / configWatcher.ts (explicit setting → folder/workspace/global precedence; absolute / workspace-relative / bare-on-PATH cases) and a timeoutMinutesResolver that reads the new setting. start() is called immediately; the service push is registered via context.subscriptions for activate-deactivate cleanup. safeRegister wraps the call so a constructor throw doesn't kill the rest of activation.

Authored src/test/suite/checkoutPollService.test.ts with 25 tests across 8 suites: (1) parseConflictRecord — 7 tests covering complete record, invalid JSON, schemaVersion mismatch, missing required field, unknown source, null-tolerant optional fields, null sessionNumber; (2) isSlotFreeForHolder H4 identity gate — 5 tests covering null orchestrator, undefined orchestrator, matching pair (same-holder), third-engine, same-engine-different-provider; (3) pollKey — 2 tests; (4) handleConflict dispatch — 3 tests covering Dismiss (no spawn), Force (spawn --force), in-flight de-dup (second handleConflict short-circuits without re-prompting); (5) beginPolling state machine — 4 tests covering immediate-retry-on-slot-free, no-spawn-when-held-by-third, session-number arg presence/absence, dispose() halts watchers + clears state; (6) processFile ingest — 2 tests covering happy-path consume+dispatch + malformed-JSON drop; (7) conflictDirPath; (8) prompt-action constants. All 25 pass via npm run test:unit (Mocha + vscode-stub). Full suite: 446 pass + 2 pre-existing failures (configEditor-foundation + notificationsSection, unchanged from S2/S3/S4).

Authored src/test/playwright/checkout-polling.spec.ts. PASSING scenario: 'CheckoutPollService consumes a pre-existing conflict sentinel on activation' — scaffolds a session set, sets HOME + USERPROFILE to a tmpdir-scoped override, drops a conflict sentinel at <homeOverride>/.dabbler/checkout-conflicts/test-sentinel.json, launches VS Code, opens the Session Sets view (triggering extension activation → safeRegister → CheckoutPollService.start() → directory scan), waits up to 15s for the sentinel file to be deleted by the service. Asserts deletion AND that the session-state.json's orchestrator block is unchanged (consume surfaces a prompt; it does NOT silently force-override). Runtime: 12.6s on Windows + Playwright _electron.launch. Hit one debug iteration: npx playwright test invoked directly doesn't run the compile step (only `npm run test:playwright` chains compile + tsc --outDir out + playwright); after running `npm run compile` manually the test passes deterministically. Documented in inline comments. SKIPPED scenario with FIXME: 'second orchestrator polls, holder closes, second orchestrator auto-attaches' — the full happy path requires clicking the 'Poll for release' button on a VS Code information-message toast, which runs into the same cross-iframe brittleness session-sets-tree.spec.ts + S4 release-checkout palette scenario hit. Layer-2 covers the polling state machine end-to-end (4 beginPolling tests asserting on the spawned start_session args, the retry-on-state-change path, dispose semantics). Manual operator smoke remains the path for the toast-button UX.

Authored verify_session5.py following the Session 1+2+3 template (RouteResult JSON-dump-first per feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling). Bundled the primary deliverables in full (CheckoutPollService.ts, claude-session-start-invoker.js, configWatcher.ts, checkoutPollService.test.ts, checkout-polling.spec.ts) plus extension.ts excerpt (service registration block + pythonPath/timeout resolvers) plus package.json excerpt (the new dabblerSessionSets.checkoutPollTimeoutMinutes setting) plus three ground-truth references (proposal-addendum §9 H3+H4 verdicts, spec.md Session 5 only, start_session.py EXIT_CHECKOUT_CONFLICT + boundary checks excerpt). Bundle 78,491 chars — between S1's 56k and S2's 96k. Seven assertion groups (A conflict-record contract, B service state machine, C H4 identity gate, D detection-path correctness, E extension registration robustness, F test adequacy, G risky/missing). Round A verdict: VERIFIED with no must-fix issues. Round B not required. Session 5 closes near top of $0.10-$0.25 forecast band ($0.032 single round); cumulative routed spend across S1+S2+S3+S5 ≈$0.165 of $1.25 total Set 033 NTE.

ai_router/session_state.py: _flip_state_to_closed now sets state["orchestrator"] = None unconditionally just before the snapshot file write. The assignment lives after the forceClosed branch and before json.dump, so every path through the function (mid-set close, final close, forced incident-recovery close) clears the orchestrator block. Idempotent: a block that was already None lands the same write. The clear is universal across any caller of _flip_state_to_closed — new writer paths (a hypothetical force-recover CLI, a repair path that flips a snapshot, etc.) inherit the check-in without opting in. Inline comment expanded post-Round-A per the gemini-pro nice-to-have suggestion to be more emphatic about the unconditional propagation.

ai_router/tests/test_close_session_snapshot_flip.py: three new tests under a Set 033 Session 6 banner — (a) test_close_session_clears_orchestrator_block_on_final_close exercises the gate-running close_session.run() on a 1-session set and asserts the post-close orchestrator block is None; (b) test_close_session_clears_orchestrator_block_on_mid_set_close runs a session-1-of-5 mid-set close (no change-log.md; SET stays in-progress; orchestrator block clears), pinning the per-session-not-per-set release contract; (c) test_close_session_orchestrator_clear_is_idempotent_tier_agnostic invokes _flip_state_to_closed directly on two synthetic state files (block populated, block already None) and asserts both end at orchestrator: None — covers Lightweight-tier behavior via the shared writer path. ai_router/tests/e2e/test_happy_3session.py: the post-close invariant assertion now reads state.get("orchestrator") is None (was: asserting engine/model/provider/effort persisted). Full ai_router test suite: 643 pass + 1 skipped (no regressions from the writer change).

docs/session-state-schema.md: refined the existing Check-out / check-in (Set 033) section authored in S1. The Block-null invariant paragraph was rewritten to make tier symmetry + idempotence explicit (Full-tier close_session writer does it automatically; Lightweight humans write null by hand at the same boundary; already-null lands the same write). The OQ2 Documentation Aliases paragraph now surfaces the literal alias names work_checked_out / work_checked_in alongside the ledger event names work_started / closeout_succeeded; clarifies the ledger names are NOT renamed (no schema break). New Stranded-checkout recovery paragraph names both release paths (start_session --force, Release Check-Out Command Palette) and notes the writer-log audit trail.

ai_router/docs/close-out.md: three additions. (a) Section 2 (the section close_session --help echoes verbatim) gains an Orchestrator check-in paragraph after the flag-combination-rules block — names the cross-tier behavior + idempotence + pointers to the schema doc. (b) Section 3 step 9 (mark_session_complete bullet) extended to mention 'clears the orchestrator block to null' with an inline reference to Set 033 Session 6. (c) Section 4 (Common failures and remediation) gains a new Stranded check-out (Set 033 Session 6) failure pattern naming the two recovery paths and clarifying the H3 refusal fires at the NEXT start_session, not at close-out (close-out never sees the stranded state). The latter recovery paragraph addresses the spec's Section 5 mention — note the spec author labeled it Section 5 but the actual failure-modes section in close-out.md is Section 4.

docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md: new Orchestrator check-out / check-in (Set 033) subsection inserted under Session-Set Lifecycle and State File, between Hand-authored session-set folders and Cancelling and restoring. Codifies the within-set sequential invariant (at most one in-progress session per set) AND the across-set parallel invariant (different sets can each have their own in-progress session), names force-override as the one explicit deviation, states H4 holder identity as engine + provider composite, calls out tier symmetry. Switching Orchestrators Between Sessions section extended with a brief pointer at the new subsection so the two surfaces don't drift.

docs/cross-repo-checkout-notice.md (new file): one-time copy source for the three consumer repos' CLAUDE.md files (dabbler-platform, dabbler-access-harvester, dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb). Self-contained snippet between horizontal-rule lines: introduces the check-out / check-in model, names the within-set sequential + across-set parallel invariants, shows the H3 refusal-error body verbatim, names the three forward paths (wait / --force / Release Check-Out), states cross-tier behavior, surfaces the OQ2 alias names, points at the canonical references via absolute GitHub URLs (master). Notes-for-the-paster section covers per-consumer adjustments + the Lightweight-tier (dabbler-homehealthcare-accessdb) caveat. Operator pulls into each consumer manually; no PRs from this repo.

docs/session-sets/033-orchestrator-checkout-checkin-implementation/change-log.md (new file): final-session aggregation across S1-S6 per the [[project_final_session_changelog_pre_close]] pattern. Top context block points at the Set 032 audit verdicts + this set's six-session split. One section per session capturing what shipped (writer / reader / UI / tests / queueing / cross-tier close-out + docs + release). Closing sections: 'What ships across the framework' (one-paragraph summary of the user-facing surface), 'Risks closed' (R1-R7 from the spec, status), 'Follow-ups out of scope' (pre-existing S2 accordion-body bug, Set 034/035 audit candidate).

pyproject.toml: 0.5.1 -> 0.6.0. tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/package.json: 0.17.1 -> 0.18.0. ai_router/CHANGELOG.md: new 0.6.0 entry covering Added (start_session H3 gate, --force flag, +2 nested timestamps, cross-tier check-in with idempotence), Changed (H4 holder-identity composite, OQ2 doc aliases), Migration (pre-0.6.0 in-flight tolerance, stranded-checkout recovery). tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/CHANGELOG.md: new 0.18.0 entry covering Added (releaseCheckOut command, polling/queueing service, timeout setting, multi-in-progress rendering), Changed (command rename, listInProgressSets resolver, Claude hook -> invoker), Removed (.dabbler/orchestrator.json marker), Layer-3 tests, Migration (cross-repo notice path). CLAUDE.md: Extension versioning walk extended with 0.18.0 entry + Current line bumped + companion 0.6.0 PyPI reference.

Authored verify_session6.py following the S1-S5 template (RouteResult JSON-dump-first per [[feedback_ai_router_route_result_handling]]). Bundled the writer change (session_state.py excerpt) + three new tests + e2e assertion update + all three doc changes + cross-repo notice + change-log + version-bump artifacts (pyproject.toml, package.json, both CHANGELOG entries, CLAUDE.md version walk) + Session 6 spec contract + proposal-addendum §9 verdicts. Bundle 72,424 chars. Eight assertion groups (A writer correctness, B test contract pinning, C-E doc fidelity for schema/close-out/workflow, F cross-repo notice self-containment, G version-bumps consistency, H risky/missing edge cases). Round A verdict: VERIFIED with no must-fix issues. dabbler-ai-router 0.6.0 published to PyPI (https://pypi.org/project/dabbler-ai-router/0.6.0/). Build via .venv/Scripts/python.exe -m build; upload via .venv/Scripts/python.exe -m twine upload dist/dabbler_ai_router-0.6.0*. Two-step issue resolution on auth: first attempt 403 Forbidden because the operator's $env:PYPI_USERNAME was not set to the literal '__token__' that PyPI's token-based auth requires; resolved by overriding TWINE_USERNAME=__token__ explicitly while keeping the token in $env:PYPI_AIROUTER_API_TOKEN. Note for the operator's future-release env: setting PYPI_USERNAME=__token__ (or aliasing TWINE_USERNAME to that value) would let twine pick credentials up automatically without the override. Also: an unrelated incident during the publish prep — a buggy bash idiom leaked the Marketplace PAT to stdout; operator rotated the PAT in Azure DevOps and the Marketplace publish (Step 11) is deferred until they confirm the new token is in place. Memory entry [[feedback_no_env_var_probing]] captures the lesson.

VS Code Marketplace publish of DarndestDabbler.dabbler-ai-orchestration 0.18.0 DEFERRED. Reason: the buggy env-var probe earlier in the session leaked the AZURE_VSCODE_MARKETPLACE_TOKEN to stdout. Operator chose 'rotate now; defer Marketplace publish' so the leaked PAT could be revoked in Azure DevOps before any publish attempt with the new PAT. Version artifacts (package.json 0.18.0, CHANGELOG entry, CLAUDE.md version walk) are committed locally and shipped via the Session 6 implementation commit. The publish itself is a follow-on action the operator runs when ready: cd tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration && npx vsce package && npx vsce publish --pat $env:AZURE_VSCODE_MARKETPLACE_TOKEN (per [[reference_vsce_pat]]). Not a Set 033 blocker — close_session can proceed without it because the gates don't include 'Marketplace published'.
